sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Network OPEX Optimisation (Maintenance / Energy / Field Ops)
condition_precedents: Network inventory accurately mapped in OSS / NMS with asset utilisation data
Vendor managed services contracts reviewed for performance SLAs and penalties
Energy management and free-cooling programme initiated for passive infrastructure
industry_sources: TRAI — Annual Report 2023-24 (trai.gov.in/release-publication/reports) — telecom network OPEX benchmarks India
GSMA — Mobile Economy India 2024 (gsma.com/solutions-and-impact/connectivity/mobile-economy/) — network cost structure and efficiency benchmarks
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Disaggregate network OPEX by cost driver: passive infrastructure (power, rent), active network (maintenance, spares), and managed services; benchmark vs revenue
Identify top-5 cost reduction levers: tower power cost reduction, network equipment refresh, RAN-sharing, NOC automation, and spares pooling
Execute quick wins (automated energy management, spares pooling); develop business case for medium-term investments (equipment refresh, RAN sharing)
Track network OPEX as % of revenue and EBITDA, and cost per subscriber monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Network OPEX as % of service revenue >25% vs peer operator benchmark of <22%
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Structural OPEX reduction will improve EBITDA margin

diagnostic_signals.signal: Power cost >35% of total passive infrastructure OPEX with diesel backup >20% of power mix
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Energy management and green energy transition will reduce power cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Managed service contract for RAN management not benchmarked for >3 years
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Managed service rebid will improve pricing and SLA

required_data_fields: Network OPEX breakdown by cost category and geography
Power consumption data: grid vs diesel ratio per tower/BTS
Network equipment age profile and maintenance cost
RAN managed service contract terms and SLA performance
TRAI network performance indicators (CAGR trends, EBITDA benchmarks)

sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Passive Infrastructure Sharing / Tower Co Model
condition_precedents: Tower asset inventory and ownership structure validated
Tower sharing agreement (TSA) negotiated with preferred InfraCo partners
Regulatory clearance obtained for passive sharing under TRAI guidelines
industry_sources: TRAI — Infrastructure Sharing in Telecom (trai.gov.in/release-publication/reports) — tower sharing benefits and benchmarks India
GSMA — Tower Sharing Economics (gsma.com) — passive infrastructure sharing cost savings 15-35%
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Audit tower portfolio: segment by tenancy ratio, location type, and EBITDA contribution; identify low-tenancy towers for hosting commercial sharing
Approach alternate operators for co-location on strategic towers; negotiate standard TRAI-compliant sharing agreements with market-rate GLR (Ground Level Rent) pass-through
Evaluate sale-and-leaseback of non-core towers to infrastructure investor (IndiGrid, ATC, Brookfield) to unlock capital and reduce opex
Track tenancy ratio, co-location revenue per tower, and passive infrastructure cost per subscriber monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Tenancy ratio <1.3× for >50% of tower portfolio vs industry leader benchmark of >2.0×
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Sharing agreements will monetise underutilised tower capacity

diagnostic_signals.signal: Passive infrastructure cost (rent, power, security, maintenance) >20% of total network OPEX
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Tower sharing and potential sale-leaseback will reduce passive opex per site

diagnostic_signals.signal: Tower rollout cost >₹25 L per new site where equivalent co-location on existing tower is available at <₹6 L/year lease
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Sharing preference over new build will improve return on network investment

required_data_fields: Tower inventory with tenancy ratio, location, and EBITDA per tower
Co-location agreement terms with existing sharing partners
Sale-leaseback valuation assumptions and market comparables
TRAI sharing regulations and GLR pass-through framework
Network rollout plan and new site requirements

condition_precedents: Radio access network (RAN) capacity analytics deployed
Carrier aggregation and spectrum re-farming plan approved by network team
TRAI / DOT regulatory clearance for spectrum re-farming obtained
industry_sources: TRAI — Spectrum Pricing and Efficiency Report 2024 (trai.gov.in) — spectrum utilisation benchmarks India
DoT — National Spectrum Policy (dot.gov.in) — spectrum allocation and efficiency guidelines
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Analyse spectrum utilisation by band: measure traffic load, spectral efficiency (bps/Hz), and coverage-capacity trade-offs by geography
Optimise traffic offloading: deploy small cells and Wi-Fi offload in high-density urban areas to reduce macro spectrum congestion
Identify refarming opportunities: migrate 900 MHz/1800 MHz 2G traffic to LTE; release spectrum for higher-capacity 4G/5G use
Model spectrum needs for upcoming auctions: estimate minimum auction quantity needed vs current spectral efficiency improvement trajectory

diagnostic_signals.signal: Spectral efficiency (bps/Hz) in dense urban cells <60% of theoretical maximum for deployed equipment
diagnostic_signals.confirms: RAN optimisation and traffic offloading will improve spectrum yield

diagnostic_signals.signal: 2G traffic >15% of total voice minutes on spectrum bands (900/1800 MHz) that could serve higher-value LTE/5G traffic
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Spectrum refarming will improve revenue per MHz by migrating to higher-value services

diagnostic_signals.signal: Upcoming spectrum auction costs can be partially offset by improved utilisation of current holdings
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Efficiency improvement will reduce incremental spectrum capex need

required_data_fields: Spectrum holding by band, geography, and operator
Traffic load data by cell and band (busy hour utilisation)
Spectral efficiency benchmarks by equipment vendor and technology
2G/3G traffic migration timeline and customer base
Spectrum auction schedule and estimated pricing by band

sector_specific_levers.lever_name: Customer Service Automation (IVR / Chatbot / Digital Deflection)
condition_precedents: Contact centre call driver analysis completed (top 10 reasons cover > 70% volume)
Conversational AI / chatbot platform selected and integrated with CRM
Self-service portal completion rate KPI set at > 60%
industry_sources: TRAI — Quality of Service Reports 2024 (trai.gov.in/release-publication/reports) — call centre performance benchmarks India telcos
Ardent Partners — AP Metrics That Matter 2024 (ardentpartners.com/ap-metrics-that-matter-in-2025/) — process automation benchmarks applicable to customer ops
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Analyse contact centre data: measure contact volume by reason, IVR containment rate, and cost per resolution by contact type
Design chatbot/IVR upgrade: map top-10 contact reasons to automated resolution flows; integrate with BSS for real-time account data
Deploy AI chatbot (WhatsApp, app, web) for high-volume, low-complexity contacts (bill queries, plan changes, balance inquiry); pilot with 20% of inbound volume
Track digital containment rate, cost per contact, and NPS by channel monthly; expand automation to complex contact types in Phase 2

diagnostic_signals.signal: IVR/chatbot containment rate <30% for contacts where top-10 contact reasons are automatable (bill, plan, balance)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Chatbot and IVR redesign will significantly increase automation containment

diagnostic_signals.signal: Cost per contact >₹80 via agent vs <₹10 via digital self-service for equivalent resolution
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Digital containment improvement will reduce total customer service cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Agent utilisation <70% due to high volume of simple contacts that could be self-served
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Automation will free agent capacity for complex and high-value interactions

required_data_fields: Contact volume by reason code and channel (IVR, agent, digital)
IVR containment rate by reason code
Cost per contact by channel (agent, IVR, digital)
BSS system API capability for real-time data in chatbot flows
NPS and CSAT by contact reason and resolution channel

condition_precedents: Customer lifetime value (CLV) model built by segment
Predictive churn model achieving > 70% recall deployed
Retention offer catalogue and approval workflow established
industry_sources: TRAI — Telecom Subscription Data Q4 FY2024 (trai.gov.in/release-publication/reports/telecom-subscription-data) — churn rates by operator India
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Build churn prediction model by subscriber segment: identify leading indicators (declining ARPU, reduced data usage, customer service contacts, network complaints)
Design win-back and retention programs by churn risk tier: targeted offers for high-value/high-risk subscribers; network experience improvement for service-driven churn
Activate retention programs via outbound and app push; measure response rate and 90-day retention by offer type and segment
Track monthly churn rate, ARPU of retained base, and cost per retained subscriber monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Monthly churn rate >2.5% for postpaid or >5% for prepaid vs peer operator benchmark
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Churn reduction will improve LTV and reduce subscriber acquisition cost burden

diagnostic_signals.signal: High-value subscriber (ARPU >₹500) churn rate disproportionately higher than average, indicating service quality issue for premium segment
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Targeted retention investment for high-ARPU customers will yield highest ROI

diagnostic_signals.signal: Network-related complaints (dropped calls, data speed) are primary churn driver for >30% of churned subscribers
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Network quality improvement is a prerequisite for churn reduction

required_data_fields: Subscriber-level usage data and ARPU trend (12 months)
Churn data with reason codes and churn cohort by acquisition source
Network complaint and NPS data by geography and segment
Retention offer history and response rate
Customer LTV model by segment

condition_precedents: IOT (International Outpayment Traffic) analysis completed by partner network
Bilateral IOT rates renegotiated with high-volume roaming partners
Discount roaming product launched for high-frequency travellers
industry_sources: TRAI — International Roaming Charges Report 2024 (trai.gov.in) — international roaming cost benchmarks India
GSMA — Roaming Services Report 2024 (gsma.com) — wholesale roaming pricing trends
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Analyse international roaming traffic: map volume by corridor, wholesale rate, and retail margin; identify corridors with below-market wholesale rates
Renegotiate bilateral roaming agreements with top-10 traffic corridors: benchmark vs IPX (IP eXchange) hub rates; migrate to IPX for cost efficiency
Implement real-time roaming cost control: per-session cost alerts for high-usage roamers; block inadvertent roaming on satellite or premium networks
Track wholesale roaming cost per MB/minute, retail vs wholesale margin, and roaming revenue contribution monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Roaming wholesale cost >60% of roaming retail revenue for top-3 traffic corridors
diagnostic_signals.confirms: IPX routing and bilateral renegotiation will improve roaming margin

diagnostic_signals.signal: More than 5 corridors still on legacy bilateral agreements not routed through IPX hub, paying premium per-unit rates
diagnostic_signals.confirms: IPX migration will reduce wholesale cost for high-traffic corridors

diagnostic_signals.signal: Inadvertent roaming events (satellite, premium partner network) generating bill shock and customer complaints
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Real-time controls will reduce inadvertent roaming cost and complaint volume

required_data_fields: Roaming traffic by corridor: volume (MB, minutes, SMS), wholesale cost, and retail revenue
Current bilateral agreement rates vs IPX benchmark by corridor
IPX hub connectivity and migration readiness
Inadvertent roaming incident log and cost
TRAI roaming tariff regulations

condition_precedents: Legacy BSS / OSS system landscape mapped with total cost of ownership
Cloud-native target architecture approved by CTO and CFO
Vendor selection for SaaS BSS / OSS completed (e.g. Amdocs, Ericsson BSCS)
industry_sources: GSMA — BSS/OSS Modernisation Trends 2024 (gsma.com/solutions-and-impact/technology) — cloud-native BSS/OSS migration savings 18-38%
Gartner — Market Guide for Telecom BSS 2024 (gartner.com) — BSS modernisation ROI benchmarks
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Assess BSS/OSS landscape: map all systems (billing, CRM, provisioning, mediation, network management) to vendor, age, and integration complexity
Develop modernisation roadmap: prioritise by business impact (revenue leakage, time-to-market, customer experience) and technical risk; select cloud-native vs SaaS replacement
Execute migration in phases starting with mediation and billing (revenue impact); implement API gateway for legacy integration; decompose monolith to microservices
Track IT OPEX as % of revenue, time-to-market for new plan launch, and billing accuracy rate monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: New product/plan time-to-market >30 days due to BSS configuration complexity vs cloud-native benchmark of <7 days
diagnostic_signals.confirms: BSS modernisation will improve time-to-market and commercial agility

diagnostic_signals.signal: IT OPEX for BSS/OSS maintenance >12% of total revenue vs cloud-native operator benchmark of <7%
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Modernisation will reduce IT maintenance cost through platform consolidation

diagnostic_signals.signal: Revenue leakage from billing errors or unmediated traffic >0.5% of total revenue
diagnostic_signals.confirms: BSS modernisation will close revenue assurance gaps

required_data_fields: BSS/OSS landscape: system inventory, vendor, age, and annual maintenance cost
Time-to-market data for recent product launches
Revenue assurance report with leakage estimate
IT OPEX breakdown (BSS/OSS maintenance, licensing, managed services)
Cloud-native or SaaS BSS vendor options and migration feasibility

condition_precedents: Per-site energy consumption data available for > 80% of sites
Free-cooling and smart HVAC controls deployed at high-power sites
Renewable energy or DG-to-solar transition plan approved
industry_sources: TRAI — Annual Report 2023-24 (trai.gov.in/release-publication/reports) — energy consumption benchmarks for Indian telecom networks
CEA — Annual Report 2023-24 (cea.nic.in/annual-report/) — energy cost benchmarks for telecom infrastructure
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Measure energy consumption per site: electricity units consumed per BTS/tower per month; calculate energy per GB traffic and trend YoY
Deploy free cooling, battery energy storage systems (BESS), and solar at high-consumption sites; negotiate green energy PPA for grid supply
Implement remote BTS (intelligent energy controller) and passive cooling optimisation (insulation, variable-speed fans) at all managed sites
Track energy cost per GB traffic, diesel % in energy mix, and carbon intensity monthly; report against SBTi targets

diagnostic_signals.signal: Diesel generator runtime >30% of total energy supply hours, indicating grid reliability issues and high diesel cost
diagnostic_signals.confirms: BESS and solar will reduce diesel dependency and fuel cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Energy cost per GB traffic growing >10% YoY due to traffic growth without corresponding energy efficiency improvement
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Energy efficiency programme will decouple energy cost from traffic growth

diagnostic_signals.signal: TowerCo SLA not optimised for energy efficiency (fixed rental structure with no energy pass-through incentive)
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Renegotiating energy-linked SLA will incentivise TowerCo energy management

required_data_fields: Energy consumption data by site (electricity units, diesel litres) per month
TowerCo energy SLA terms and diesel reimbursement structure
Solar and BESS feasibility data by site geography
Grid reliability metrics by region
Energy cost and carbon intensity targets

condition_precedents: Field service management (FSM) platform deployed with real-time GPS tracking
Job categorisation and average handling time standards established
Predictive fault analytics integrated with dispatch system
industry_sources: TRAI — Quality of Service Reports 2024 (trai.gov.in/release-publication/reports) — field workforce productivity KPIs for Indian telcos
INTERNAL_CALIBRATION — no verified external benchmark found. Savings range is internal consulting estimate.

execution_playbook.step: Analyse field force utilisation: map jobs per engineer per day, travel time vs work time, and first-time fix rate by team and geography
Deploy FSO (Field Service Optimisation) tool with AI-based dispatch, route optimisation, and remote diagnostics integration
Implement performance-linked variable pay for field force: pay on first-time fix rate, jobs completed per day, and SLA compliance
Track jobs per engineer per day, first-time fix rate, and field force cost per resolved incident monthly

diagnostic_signals.signal: Jobs per field engineer per day <4 vs FSO benchmark of 6–8 for comparable urban/rural mix and job type
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Dispatch optimisation and route planning will increase jobs completed per engineer per day

diagnostic_signals.signal: First-time fix rate <75% generating high repeat-visit cost and customer dissatisfaction
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Remote diagnostics and better parts provisioning will improve fix rate and reduce revisit cost

diagnostic_signals.signal: Travel time >40% of total field engineer productive hours due to poor territory planning or dispatch system
diagnostic_signals.confirms: Route optimisation will reduce travel time and increase productive work hours

required_data_fields: Field force utilisation data: jobs per engineer per day, travel time, and work time
First-time fix rate and repeat-visit rate by job type and team
Territory allocation and customer concentration data
FSO tool options and integration with workforce management system
Field engineer compensation structure and variable pay policy

source_verification_note: industry_sources entries indicate the published works that directionally support each lever's savings thesis. Where no external benchmark was found, INTERNAL_CALIBRATION is noted. All cited sources are real publications; specific savings percentages remain internal calibrations unless the source directly cites a matching range.
